title: SCMP: China deploys Shandong carrier group to South China Sea amid regional tensions
summary: South China Morning Post reports the PLA Navy Shandong carrier strike group entered the South China Sea, joining an expanded naval deployment across multiple maritime theaters.
contents:
# China Deploys Shandong Carrier Group to South China Sea

## Deployment Report
The South China Morning Post reported on May 1, 2026, that the PLA Navy Shandong carrier strike group entered the South China Sea, marking a simultaneous two-carrier deployment with the Liaoning operating in the Philippine Sea.

## Force Details
- CV-17 Shandong (aircraft carrier)
- Escort formation including Type 055 and Type 052D destroyers
- Support vessels for sustained operations

## Strategic Significance
The simultaneous deployment of both operational carrier groups represents the most significant PLAN power projection demonstration of 2026. Regional security officials characterize the deployment as "more provocative than previously observed."

## Regional Response
- Taiwan raised combat preparedness levels
- Japan tracking Liaoning through southwestern waters
- Philippines monitoring increased naval presence near disputed features
- US 7th Fleet maintaining awareness operations

## Tags
china, plan, shandong, south-china-sea, carrier-deployment, power-projection
